mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in unemployment rate above 4.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Either GDPC1 or HOUST shows negative growth while SPX returns remain positive over 60 days.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening supply-side capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns despite a 2%+ decline in INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3% (CPIAUCSL) triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) signal a monetary tightening cycle, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns despite both FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL > 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 150k indicates robust labor market conditions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns despite PAYEMS exceeding 150k.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong demand-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Either RSAFS or HOUST shows negative growth while SPX returns remain positive over 20 days.
